structured.description: A 3D rendering engine using LWJGL and OpenGL, with a focus on creating a first-person camera and a world filled with blocks. The code initializes the display, sets up the GL context, loads a default shader, and creates a model for the camera and a texture for the blocks. It then enters an infinite loop where it updates the position of the world and renders the scene using the shader and camera.

items.description: in the provided Java file is responsible for initializing and setting up the display and graphics hardware, creating a camera and shader, loading a texture, and rendering a 3D world. The class also includes a loop that updates the position of the camera and renders the 3D world at each frame, and provides an exit mechanism to terminate the program.
items.name: Main

items.description: initializes display and GL resources, then enters an infinite loop for rendering and exiting with a successful status code of 0.

params.name: args
params.description:
1 or more command line arguments passed to the `main` method when the program is executed directly from the command line, and are used by the program to determine how it should behave.

* Length: The function takes an array of strings as input, which is denoted by the variable `args`.
* Elements: `args` contains multiple elements or command-line arguments passed to the program.
* Data types: Each element in `args` is a string, indicating that each argument is a text value.

items.name: main

items.description: sets up a display mode and creates a GL context with forward compatibility and core profile, enabling vsync. It also prints the GL version string.
items.name: initDisplay

items.description: initializes various graphics settings and sets up a 3D camera, texture, and vertices for rendering a 3D world. It also loads a shader and creates a world object.
items.name: initGL

items.description: runs a loop of rendering and updating the display using a timer to measure frame rate, free memory, and overall system resources.
items.name: loop

items.description: updates the position of an object `w` based on input data `dt` and transforms the position using a matrix `c.getTransform()`.

params.name: dt
params.description: delta time since the last frame and is used to update the position of the object in the game world.

items.name: update

items.description: renders a 3D model using the provided camera and viewport dimensions, utilizing uniform float values for color and matrix multiplication for transforming the model's vertices.
items.name: render

items.description: terminates the current Java process with the specified exit status, which can range from 0 to 255.

params.name: status
params.description: exit code that will be used to terminate the Java process when the `exit()` function is called.

items.name: exit
